strategies: pair each proof statement with a bounded list of valid possible reasons before independent construction
compare matched examples where the same claim needs different reasons in different contexts
use claim-reason sorting routines before full proof writing
annotate givens, target conclusion, and needed intermediate results before writing proof steps
use teacher think-alouds that name why each subclaim is needed in the argument chain
compare complete proofs with scrambled-step versions and reconstruct the logic order
require theorem, definition, or property support for every non-given statement
compare visually plausible but unjustified claims with actually justified steps
use “what do we know” versus “what looks true” routines during proof review
use bounded mixed proofs that isolate one algebraic substitution or equality step at a time
label whether each step is algebra-driven or geometry-driven while preserving the same claim chain
compare correct and broken mixed proofs where the algebra no longer supports the geometry
